fact: AI in this project is clinician-assistive only. It must not act as an autonomous diagnosis, prescribing, discharge, or emergency-triage engine.
Symptom-analysis features must use deterministic red-flag rules plus retrieved medical guidance, not pure free-form LLM output.
Every AI output must be reviewable, editable, rejectable, and auditable by the clinician.
AI requests must run server-side only and use the minimum necessary patient data.
Track model version, prompt version, retrieval sources, input snapshot, output snapshot, and clinician accept-edit-reject outcomes for AI interactions.
Do not ship a public patient-facing symptom checker in v1. First validate clinician-assistive workflows with staged evaluation.
Offline evaluation, clinician review, silent mode, and limited pilot rollout should happen before broad AI deployment.
